 - uniqueness: it is the only object with a given prooperty.
 - uniqueness complement: it is the only object *without* a given property.
 - centrality: it is a central point around which a domain is symmetric.
 - extremeness: it is an object that has the largest or the smallest feature among all the others.
 Now, you have to prioritise the selection of the only object that satisfies one of the above mentioned properties, i.e., it is the only object with that property in the list. If there are multiple objects that satisfy one or more of the above mentioned properties, select the one that is unique, then extreme, then central, and eventually unique complement. Return the option you chose, without the score, between <answer></answer> tags.

2 uniqueness complement this refers to an option being the only one without a given property all options except 1 have the property of being 10 making 1 unique in not having this property but this is more about lacking a common trait among others rather than having a singular trait


3 centrality this would refer to an option that serves as a central point around which the domain is symmetric given the linear nature of the options a list of numbers there isnt a clear central point in terms of symmetry since the options dont form a symmetric set around any middle value


4 extremeness this refers to an object that has the largest or smallest feature among all others option 10 is the largest and option 1 is the smallest


given these definitions we prioritize as follows


 unique 1 is unique because its the only number less than 10
 extreme both 1 smallest and 10 largest satisfy this but since 1 is already identified as unique it takes precedence
 central there isnt a clear central option given the nature of the list
 unique complement while 1 could also be seen as unique in lacking the property of being 10 like all other options this is secondary to its uniqueness


therefore prioritizing uniqueness first the option that stands out is 1 because its the only option less than 10 making it unique among the provided choices
